---
title: "Cheatsheet · DAG vocabulary"
subtitle: "A quick reference for causal structure"
---

::: {.cheatsheet}

## Roles of variables

[Exposure (A).]{.lead-in} The treatment of interest. &nbsp;
[Outcome (Y).]{.lead-in} The endpoint. &nbsp;
[Confounder (L).]{.lead-in} Common cause of A and Y — **adjust for it**. &nbsp;
[Mediator (M).]{.lead-in} On the path A → Y — **adjust with care**. &nbsp;
[Collider (C).]{.lead-in} Caused by two variables — **do not adjust**.

## The three structures

| Structure | Shape | Conditioning on the middle |
|---|---|---|
| Chain | X → Y → Z | **blocks** the path |
| Fork | X ← Y → Z | **blocks** the path |
| Collider | X → Y ← Z | **opens** the path (bias!) |

::: {.pullquote}
Conditioning on a collider *creates* a spurious association. Among students
admitted to an elite school, ability and wealth look negatively related — because
admission is a collider.
:::

## Adjustment

[Backdoor path.]{.lead-in} A non-causal path A ← … → Y. Block them all. &nbsp;
[Adjustment set.]{.lead-in} Variables that block every backdoor path (e.g. {L}). &nbsp;
[Disjunctive cause criterion.]{.lead-in} Adjust for any cause of A **or** Y, excluding descendants of A (VanderWeele & Shpitser, 2011).

## Building a DAG (5 steps)

1. List variables (exposure, outcome, plausible causes).
2. Order them in time.
3. Draw an arrow from each cause to its direct effect.
4. Check roles (confounder / mediator / collider).
5. Review for omitted causes — then iterate.

::: {.risk}
Risk: a missing confounder cannot be recovered by any algorithm. "You cannot
discover what you did not measure."
:::

## Discovery assumptions

[Markov condition.]{.lead-in} Each variable ⟂ its non-descendants given its parents. &nbsp;
[Faithfulness.]{.lead-in} Dependencies reflect structure (no cancellations). &nbsp;
[Causal sufficiency.]{.lead-in} No unmeasured common causes.

**Core rule:** if X ⟂ Y | Z, there is no direct edge between X and Y.

:::

*Print this page (⌘/Ctrl-P) for a two-column handout.* See also the
[full glossary](../glossary.qmd).
